text: Yes, there is a detection of 'whack-a-mole' risk when solving Type 4 problems with Type 1, 2, or 3 thinking. This is noted in the context that "synergies and 'whack-a-mole' effects... can result from a highly effective policy design" (p. 26). Additionally, the framework emphasizes that biases can lead to inconsistent policies, potentially reflecting the 'whack-a-mole' issue (p. 26).
